summary: P1 Production Infrastructure testing completed. All backend APIs working: GET /api/health (includes cache, storage, sentry status), GET /api/infra/status (infrastructure overview), POST /api/infra/cache/clear (admin-only), POST /api/infra/cache/clear/{namespace} (admin-only). Cache integration verified on /api/subscriptions/plans (5min TTL) and /api/admin/analytics/comprehensive (1min TTL). Non-admin users correctly receive 403 on cache clear endpoints. Frontend ErrorBoundary component properly wraps entire app in App.js. Backend starts cleanly with all new services.
critical_code_review_comments: Good: CacheService singleton with namespace-based TTL caches (plans:5min, stats:1min, user:2min)
Good: Cache stats expose hit_count, miss_count, hit_rate for monitoring
Good: StorageService gracefully degrades to local when S3 not configured
Good: TaskManager enhanced with retry logic (max 3 retries), priority queue, and timeout handling
Good: Sentry init_sentry handles missing DSN gracefully (logs info message, returns False)
Good: _sentry_before_send filter scrubs Authorization/Cookie headers and drops 404 errors
Good: infra_routes properly uses db injection pattern (set_db) matching other routes
Good: Admin check in infra_routes queries db.users for role (not Pydantic model)
Good: ErrorBoundary component has proper getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch
Good: ErrorBoundary attempts Sentry capture in componentDidCatch when configured
context_for_next_testing_agent: P1 Production Infrastructure fully tested. 1) CacheService: Singleton in /app/backend/services/cache_service.py with namespaced TTL caches (plans, pricing, stats, user, crypto, health, default). @cached decorator available for async functions. 2) StorageService: /app/backend/services/storage_service.py - uses S3 when AWS_S3_BUCKET/AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY configured, else local /tmp/notary_uploads. 3) TaskManager: /app/backend/services/task_manager.py - enhanced with JobStatus enum, JobPriority enum, retry logic (max 3), timeout handling, exponential backoff. 4) Infrastructure Routes: /api/infra/status (any auth user), /api/infra/cache/clear (admin only), /api/infra/cache/clear/{namespace} (admin only). 5) Sentry: init_sentry in middleware/security.py, capture_sentry_error helper, before_send filter. 6) Cache integrated into: GET /api/subscriptions/plans (plans namespace, 5min TTL), GET /api/admin/analytics/comprehensive (stats namespace, 1min TTL). 7) Frontend ErrorBoundary wraps entire app in App.js.

infra_status.GET /api/infra/status: WORKING - returns cache stats, storage status, jobs stats, sentry config, infra version
infra_status.cache_namespaces: plans (5min/32), pricing (5min/32), stats (1min/128), user (2min/512), crypto (1min/64), health (30s/8), default (2min/256)

cache_clear.POST /api/infra/cache/clear: WORKING - Admin only, clears all caches
cache_clear.POST /api/infra/cache/clear/{namespace}: WORKING - Admin only, clears specific namespace

cache_integration.GET /api/subscriptions/plans: WORKING - First call miss, subsequent calls hit from 'plans' namespace

sentry_integration.graceful_handling: WORKING - init_sentry returns False, logs info message

error_boundary.integration: WORKING - Wraps entire app in App.js (lines 37-185)
error_boundary.fallback_ui: Error screen with Shield icon, 'Try Again' and 'Go Home' buttons
